topic.what: Atomic decision boundary as structural requirement for guaranteeing execution-time admissibility
topic.how: Labeled transition system formalism distinguishing atomic systems (evaluation+transition coupled) from split systems (evaluation and transition separate)
topic.why_matters: Autonomous agents execute irreversible state transitions; existing governance mechanisms cannot guarantee admissibility at execution moment due to decision-execution gap

characteristics.what_it_measures: Conditions under which split systems provably fail
characteristics.context: Applies to all split systems under adversarial concurrent environment

characteristics.evidence: Table 1: RBAC, ABAC, OPA, Cedar, AWS IAM, Kubernetes Admission
characteristics.context: Structural classification independent of expressiveness or performance
Escalate outcome extends classical binary model
Non-equivalence theorem with constructive witness trace

novelty: Atomic decision boundary definition (indivisible LTS step coupling evaluation and transition)
Non-equivalence theorem with constructive counterexample trace
Escalation closure requirement: escalation transfers atomicity obligation to supervisor
Structural taxonomy of 6 governance mechanisms as atomic/split/partially-atomic
open_problems: Implementing atomic boundary in practice (hardware, database, protocol)
Behavioral drift detection above enforcement layer (Paper 2)
